title: Misclassification Classification and Evaluation Triage
description:
## Summary
Add a first-class misclassification-classification layer to evaluation analysis so incorrect items are classified by what kind of problem they represent, rather than assuming every error is fixable by score optimization.

## Scope
- Each misclassified item is classified into exactly one of four operator-facing categories: `score_configuration_problem`, `information_gap`, `guideline_gap_requires_sme`, or `mechanical_malfunction`.
- Evaluations also gain evaluation-level red flags for cross-item mechanical/pathological patterns that are not item-local, such as all predictions collapsing to one class or score results containing execution errors.
- The dashboard evaluation view gains a visual breakdown of the misclassification categories plus item-level drill-down.
- This work remains distinct from deterministic reference datasets and distinct from RCA reliability/contract work.

## Key assumptions
- Feedback contradictions remain a separate report/workstream and are not folded into the per-item four-category classifier.
- The new classification layer becomes part of the broader RCA pipeline, not an unrelated side feature.
- Public-facing web app documentation is required as part of this feature.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
## Design boundaries

This feature is a first-class evaluation-analysis layer, not just an extension of optimization RCA.

### Core decisions
- Per-item classification is forced into exactly four operator-facing categories:
  - `score_configuration_problem`
  - `information_gap`
  - `guideline_gap_requires_sme`
  - `mechanical_malfunction`
- Evaluation-level red flags are a separate layer for non-item-local problems such as class collapse or execution-error patterns.
- The dashboard should show both:
  - an overall breakdown visualization for the evaluation
  - item-level classification details on individual misclassifications
- Feedback contradictions / label inconsistency remain a separate report/workstream and are not folded into this classifier.

### Intended operator outcome
The feature should help operators decide whether an evaluation suggests:
- score optimization work
- upstream data/transcript remediation
- SME/rubric clarification
- mechanical/system bug investigation

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Epic implementation complete: backend classification taxonomy + summary flags + recommendation gating, first-class misclassification payload exposure in evaluation info, evaluation detail segmented breakdown + red-flag alerts + item-level drill-down, and public docs updates. Validation executed across pytest + dashboard build + dashboard UI tests.
